title: Packages, Repositories, Dependencies, and Software Provenance · System Lens: System Project Dependencies
summary: Install or update a required tool while preserving repository trust, version evidence, rollback options, and the boundary between system and project dependencies. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux system project dependencies increment in a distribution-aware package change record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate distribution packages, language/project environments, containers, and manually installed binaries by ownership and update path.
Evaluate repository ownership, signing keys, transport, scope, pinning, and update policy before adding a source.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Predict System Project Dependencies
steps.instruction:
theory case — Host proposes lab-tool 5.4.1 from a signed distribution repository and 5.5.0 from an unverified script. Inspect origin, dependencies, installed files, service impact, and rollback before choosing.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the System Project Dependencies contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate distribution packages, language/project environments, containers, and manually installed binaries by ownership and update path.

content.text: Host proposes lab-tool 5.4.1 from a signed distribution repository and 5.5.0 from an unverified script. Inspect origin, dependencies, installed files, service impact, and rollback before choosing.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which System Project Dependencies condition decided your prediction.

steps.title: Bound System Project Dependencies
steps.instruction:
theory case — Host proposes lab-tool 6.4.1 from a signed distribution repository and 6.5.0 from an unverified script. Inspect origin, dependencies, installed files, service impact, and rollback before choosing.

After tracing the worked code, choose the System Project Dependencies application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate distribution packages, language/project environments, containers, and manually installed binaries by ownership and update path.

content.text: Separate distribution packages, language/project environments, containers, and manually installed binaries by ownership and update path. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Capture identity, path, process, file, and service state; predict parsing and scope; run one safe simulated observation; interpret exit status; then change one bounded layer and verify rollback.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Host proposes lab-tool 5.4.1 from a signed distribution repository and 5.5.0 from an unverified script. Inspect origin, dependencies, installed files, service impact, and rollback before choosing. A defensible solution applies System Project Dependencies, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a distribution-aware package change record.

content.code:

id
stat README.md

content.caption: Runnable safe terminal evidence example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Installing every dependency system-wide makes projects more reproducible. This fails the System Project Dependencies contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Repair the System Project Dependencies defect

A teammate acts on this belief: “Installing every dependency system-wide makes projects more reproducible.” Which repair addresses the cause?
